give them the offer, further down maybe some risk reversal, presentation of price, you know, I mean typical sales letter. Now if we took that and we said instead of doing this over 50 pages, let's do it over like 10 days or 12 days or 14 days or five days or six days and do it with three pieces or maybe four pieces of content. They feel like content but their people are really being sold. And you're leading them generally there's no mention of the sale until maybe the third piece of the content. So the first piece you're setting the hook for a story, you're starting to create, you know, talk about the opportunity, getting people really excited. You're not selling them. They're like, well this guy's not selling me anything. This is awesome. And their BS detector, you know, which soon as they hit that sales letter their BS detector goes wha-ing off into the red zone, right? But when they hit that first video and you're not selling them, you're just telling them about this cool opportunity and actually giving them content, teaching them, you're really teaching them about the opportunity, you're not teaching them how to do it, you're teaching them about it. And all of a sudden that BS detector starts to relax and they start to love you and they feel great about you. And then you give them the second piece and now you take that opportunity and you show how that opportunity, how people have applied that opportunity to their lives, maybe people just like them have applied it to their lives. And now they're just really, they can't figure out what the hell's going on. And then the third piece, now you start, you basically give them more details about that opportunity. Actually give them, teach them more about what they need to do. And then usually about the two-thirds of the way through that video, you start to mention, you know, I'm, hey, I'm just really excited that so many of you have been able to check out my videos and that, you know, I've been able to help, so many of you play guitar better and I know so many of you guys are starting to play at F chord with some real authority. It's been really, it's been awesome sharing this stuff with you. And I want to tell you what I'm really excited about is I'm actually going to be creating this, this online coaching program for people that want to take to the next step and really start to put it together into songs. And I'm going to be talking about that in my next video. And so that's sort of the little switch, the little switch that, hey, you're going to be sold something. And then you come back with the sales letter or the sales video. Very, very effective. That's the sideways sales letter. Here's, that's, here's sort of the timeline. You know, you start your prelaunch, you have prelaunch content. Here's some of the triggers we'll often hit. This isn't, this isn't set in stone. We'll move these around to pan the exact offer, the, the prelaunch story. There's often a story arc that goes over the whole thing. It's almost like a novel where the excitement and the tension just builds up over time until you get to where they, where you allow them to buy from you. And so these are just some of the triggers we'll often hit. We'll often start with authority. I mean, it's how I started this presentation. I got up here and I told you I made a million and a half bucks in the last 58 hours, right? That's a, it's a really effective way to start your presentations is with authority. Then you might move into reciprocity. So now you're giving them stuff. The reciprocity is usually you're giving them content. You're teaching them. Often you're just teaching them what they need to know, what they need to do, not how to do it, right? But you're teaching them. And so that builds this reciprocity. Then your community and social proof throughout the entire launch, people start, they can see other people getting excited. Maybe you're putting up videos and people are making comments. They can see, I mean, when someone gets to one of my videos and they can see like 1100 comments people have made and like 1000 of them are, Jeff, you're awesome. I can't believe you're giving this away for free. That's a mass social proof. The real social proof. Massive. Um, then you also build it to community. Then as you get deeper into the lunch, you start to build anticipation. Usually it's best if you have some scarcity involved. You know, with this launch, that thing was open for 58 hours. They had 58 hours to buy from me. You know, after about the first 24 hours, I told them, okay, now, you know, we're gonna be closing tomorrow at midnight and it spikes sales. Nothing closes sales like a hard deadline. So that's that's a typical launch sequence. So I had this great brand or I have this great brand product launch formula, just really, really, really powerful brand for started publishing it in 2005. And it really has changed the way stuff is sold online. Literally change the way stuff is sold online in all kinds of different markets and niches. People are making a lot of the people that learn guitar niche, they used to be making, you know, couple thousand bucks a month. Then they started doing these product launches and they were making, you know, five or $6,000 of the launch. Now they're doing like 70 or $80,000 with a launch because they've really built up their market. So it's been very, very effective. So I've been teaching this since 2005 and it's been constantly updated. I open it up, let people register. I run basically I run it as a coaching program and I do this once or twice a year, basically. To my knowledge and I have pretty deep knowledge about the internet marketing world. It's the most successful product ever teaching people how to do internet marketing that was sold online. Now, you know, I hear about, you know, some of these early days doing the doing the malls and that kind of stuff and it's primarily sold offline. It's online things sold off. Those guys probably got me beat. But in terms of like my world, which is teaching internet marketing and teaching it selling it online, teaching online, I don't know anything, anyone that's done this, you know, with one product basically I've done about $17 million in sales. There's no end in sight to the awesome sauce. It just keeps on rolling along. The launches get bigger and stronger and the tentacles go out further. I've now done 12 consecutive launches that I've done at least a million dollars in sales. Half of those have done more than $3 million in sales. One of the things that I did, which I tried, I tried to keep quiet, was I actually sometimes run launches for other people. For Bill was one example. I totally suckered Bill in or maybe he suckered me in. But I was like, I think you did. But still you got to admit the sales pitch is good. He really wanted to run a launch for him. And when I'm looking to run a launch, I'm as much interested in what strategic benefit does it have to me in addition to any money I could make. Because I'm a big believer in thinking long term. And I think that's one of my competitive advantages is I always think very long term. I'm like...
